mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series models, or positive returns do not materialize when RSAFS rises alongside CPIAUCSL increases above 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 1.2 million, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation alongside a flat or rising federal funds rate forces markets to price in prolonged monetary tightening and lower equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not predict negative returns, or equity markets rally when CPIAUCSL rises with stable FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2 percent during a mid-cycle expansion confirms sustainable economic momentum and supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2 percent coincides with negative forward returns during mid-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation exceeds 4 percent.
